title: Resize World UX fixes and brush_tool orphan deletion

comment:
Pre-review findings for CR-114 (v0.1, IN_PRE_REVIEW). Document is substantively well-scoped and the technical claims I spot-checked (flow_state_app.py:109 = InputField(0,0,0,0); simulation.py resize_world calls self.clear(snapshot=False); ui/brush_tool.py exists and is not imported anywhere in flow-state) match reality. However, three defects must be corrected before pre-approval:

FINDING 1 (Section numbering, Section 9): The Implementation Plan has a structural gap. Section 9.1 (Phase 1: Test Environment Setup) is followed directly by '### 9.3 Phase 3: Implementation' -- there is no '### 9.2 Phase 2' heading at all. The parenthetical note '(Phase 2 -- RS update -- is not applicable; no RS changes.)' appears at the END of Section 9.3 but does not substitute for a properly numbered (and explicitly N/A-marked) Section 9.2. Either renumber the remaining phases (9.2 Implementation, 9.3 Qualification, ...) or insert a '### 9.2 Phase 2: Requirements (RS Update)' heading whose body explicitly states 'Not applicable -- no requirements added or modified by this CR.' Current state breaks the template's locked-section numbering.

FINDING 2 (Stale CR identifier, Sections 1, 3, 5.5): Three places reference 'CR-2026-001':
  - Section 1, item 3: '...superseded by the BrushTool in ui/tools.py:124 (CR-2026-001 ToolContext migration)...'
  - Section 3, last paragraph: '...superseded by the ToolContext-based BrushTool in ui/tools.py:124-242 (CR-2026-001 / CR-112).'
  - Section 5.5: '...superseded by the ToolContext-based BrushTool in ui/tools.py during the CR-2026-001 facade migration...'
The project does not use the 'CR-YYYY-NNN' numbering scheme; CRs are sequential 'CR-NNN'. The actual ToolContext migration CR is CR-112, which is correctly cited in the Section 12 References block ('CR-112: ToolContext Migration Completion + Documentation Reconciliation'). Replace all three 'CR-2026-001' references with 'CR-112'.

FINDING 3 (Numerical inconsistency, Section 1): Section 1 opens with 'Fix two latent defects in the Resize World feature and remove an orphan source file:' but then enumerates THREE items (1. InputField construction, 2. silent destructive clear, 3. brush_tool.py orphan). Either change 'two' to 'three' (and adjust 'and remove an orphan source file' since item 3 is already inside the list), or restructure the lead sentence so the count matches the enumeration that follows. As written, the sentence count (two) does not match the list length (three).

Additional observation (not blocking, consider for clarity): Section 8 step 1 says the field is 'pre-filled with "50.0"'. This assumes config.DEFAULT_WORLD_SIZE == 50.0 -- not established anywhere in the CR. Phrasing it as 'pre-filled with str(config.DEFAULT_WORLD_SIZE)' would be more durable to future config tweaks. Not required for this review cycle.

Reviewer assignment (per qa.md): I will assign tu_ui (UI / app / session / ui_widgets / app_controller surface) and tu_sim (engine/simulation.py resize_world change) and bu (user-visible behavior change: confirmation modal flow, destructive operation gating). tu_scene is not implicated -- no scene.py / commands.py / file_io.py / model/commands changes. tu_sketch is not implicated -- no model/ changes. Assignment will be performed after this review cycle's request-updates is addressed and the document is re-routed.

No deficiencies found. Recommending advance to pre-approval.

REJECT — malformed frontmatter.

The document has TWO YAML frontmatter blocks at the top. The first block (lines 1-3) is empty:

  ---
  {}
  ---

The second block (lines 5-8) contains the intended title and revision_summary fields. Because YAML frontmatter is parsed as the FIRST '---' delimited block at the start of the file, the QMS reads the empty {} block as the actual frontmatter. This is confirmed by 'qms status CR-114', which reports 'Title: N/A' — the system cannot see the title because the real frontmatter content is treated as document body.

Evidence verified:
- title field (frontmatter): EMPTY ({}). The 'title: Resize World UX fixes and brush_tool orphan deletion' string exists at line 6 but is OUTSIDE the parsed frontmatter block.
- revision_summary (frontmatter): EMPTY. The intended revision_summary at line 7 is also outside the parsed frontmatter.
- qms status output confirms: 'Title: N/A'.

Required correction: Remove the empty '---\n{}\n---' block at lines 1-3 so that the block at lines 5-8 becomes the file's first (and only) frontmatter block. After correction, 'qms status CR-114' should show the actual title.

Other items verified (these are clean and would otherwise pass):
- Document structure: All required CR sections present (1 Purpose, 2 Scope with 2.1/2.2/2.3, 3 Current State, 4 Proposed State, 5 Change Description with 5.1-5.5, 6 Justification, 7 Impact Assessment with 7.1-7.5, 8 Testing Summary, 9 Implementation Plan with 9.1-9.8, 10 Execution, 11 Execution Summary, 12 References).
- Section 9 numbering: correct (9.1 through 9.8 in order).
- References to CR-112 (CLOSED v2.0), CR-113 (CLOSED v2.0), SDLC-FLOW-RTM (EFFECTIVE v2.0), SDLC-FLOW-RS (EFFECTIVE v3.0): all verified valid.
- Files Affected (§2.3, §7.1) and EI table (§10) consistent with Change Description.
- No placeholder content found in pre-approval sections (1-9). Execution-time '[SUMMARY]/[Pass/Fail]/[PERFORMER]/[DATE]/[COMMENT]/[EXECUTION_SUMMARY]' placeholders in §10/§11 are correct per template.
- No TBD/TODO/FIXME found in pre-approval content.
- Parent Document field (§2.1) present: 'None'.
- Qualified State Continuity table (§7.5) present with pre/during/post rows.
- revision_summary CONTENT (when corrected to live in real frontmatter) is descriptive and accurately characterizes the procedural reset.

Per QMS-Policy zero-tolerance: any deficiency = REJECT. The frontmatter parse failure is a hard failure regardless of the otherwise clean content. Fix the duplicate frontmatter block and re-route.

Cross-references verified against codebase:

- ui_manager.py:286 fallback uses the config.scale(60), config.scale(25), str(config.DEFAULT_WORLD_SIZE) construction the CR proposes to apply at line 109.

Reviewer assignment: tu_ui (flow_state_app.py, app_controller.py, ui_widgets.py, brush_tool.py deletion), tu_sim (engine/simulation.py change), bu (user-facing visibility fix and destructive-action confirmation UX).

No typos or formatting inconsistencies found.

QA pre-review: RECOMMEND.

BU pre-review: recommend.

The CR addresses three real user-facing problems and the proposed solutions follow sound UX principles:

1. InputField visibility fix (5.1) restores access to a shipped-but-broken control. Pure win, no UX downside.

2. Confirmation dialog for destructive resize (5.3) is a strong UX improvement. The flow handles all the right cases: it warns the user with an explicit message naming what will be lost (particle count + physics-settings reset), it offers an explicit Cancel path with status feedback ('Resize cancelled'), and it skips the dialog entirely when there are zero particles to lose — avoiding click-fatigue on the harmless case. This addresses the current 'Destructive Default' red flag head-on.

3. Full physics reset (5.4) is a judgment call that I would flag as concerning if the dialog did not announce it; with the dialog message explicitly stating 'physics settings will return to defaults', the user has informed consent before the action. Combined, 5.3 and 5.4 hold up.

4. The new generic ConfirmDialog widget (5.2) sets up consistency for any future destructive action — a coherence win that pays forward.

5. Orphan deletion (5.5) has no user impact.

Minor preference (not a blocker): the CR does not specify the dialog's default-focus button. For destructive confirmations, Cancel-as-default (or no default) is the safer pattern. The dialog widget designer can settle this — flagging as a preference, not a request-updates item.

TU-SIM pre-review of CR-114 v0.1. Simulation-domain assessment.

POSITIVES (the CR is substantively sound):
1. Orphan claim verified. No imports of ui.brush_tool exist anywhere in flow-state. Active BrushTool lives at ui/tools.py:124. The orphan file's own header comment ('Replace the BrushTool class in ui/tools.py with this version') confirms it was a refactor draft never wired up. Deletion is safe.
2. Deletion scope is correctly minimal — engine/particle_brush.py (which the orphan imports) is genuinely live: used by core/scene.py:35,82 and surfaced through the ToolContext brush facade. Not orphaned by this change.
3. The semantic argument for full physics reset on resize is sound. The current resize_world() leaves an unprincipled middle state — particles wiped but gravity/damping/sigma/etc. retained from the prior session. 'Resize the world means a fresh canvas' is the right contract.
4. Order of operations in §5.4 (reset() then assign new_size) is correct and self-flagged.

DEFECT IN PROPOSED CODE (must fix before approval):

§5.4's resize_world() patch will push TWO snapshots onto undo_stack per resize, because reset() at simulation.py:290-303 internally calls self.snapshot() at line 292. The proposed code is:

    self.snapshot()                # outer snapshot
    if new_size < 10.0: new_size = 10.0
    self.reset()                   # internal snapshot AGAIN
    self.world_size = new_size

Behavioral consequence: Ctrl+Z after a resize will require two presses to actually revert, and the first undo restores a state identical to the pre-resize state (since the inner snapshot fires before any reset mutation occurs). This is a user-visible defect in the very feature the CR is fixing.

Recommended fix — remove the outer snapshot() call and rely on reset()'s internal snapshot:

    if new_size < 10.0: new_size = 10.0
    self.reset()                   # snapshots, then restores defaults
    self.world_size = new_size

This is a one-line edit to §5.4 and the corresponding code block. Please verify on the bench during EI-6 by checking len(sim.undo_stack) before/after a resize_world() call.

OUT-OF-SCOPE OBSERVATION (not blocking — flagging for backlog):

Neither clear() nor reset() zeros the tether arrays (tether_entity_idx, tether_local_pos, tether_stiffness) or joint_ids, force_x/y, last_x/y. After a reset(), if particles are spawned into slots [0..count] that previously had non-default tether/joint values, those values persist as stale data. Today this is masked because Compiler.rebuild() rewrites these fields when invoked. But a resize_world() followed by user particle painting (no compiler trigger) could expose stale tether state on those slots.

This is a latent Simulation-domain hygiene issue that pre-dates CR-114 and is out of scope here. Recommend logging as a backlog item for a future CR (possibly bundled with any future tether/joint refactor). I do not require it be addressed in CR-114.

DOMAIN INTEGRITY: The change respects the Sketch/Simulation separation. resize_world() and reset() remain pure-physics-domain operations. No Numba kernel changes. No data-oriented design violations. The two-way Compiler coupling is unaffected.

VERDICT: request-updates. The single-line snapshot duplication in §5.4 must be corrected; the fix is trivial. Once that is addressed I expect to recommend on the next cycle.

TU-UI pre-review: RECOMMEND (with one observation for executor awareness).

Verified the technical claims against the live code:
- flow_state_app.py:109 -- confirmed InputField(0,0,0,0) attached to Session.input_world.
- ui_manager.py:283-286 -- confirmed truthy app_input_world short-circuits the well-formed fallback. Root cause diagnosis is correct.
- app_controller.py:180-187 -- current action_resize_world matches the 'Before' snippet in section 5.3 exactly. Proposed split into action_resize_world + _do_resize_world is consistent with how AppController already exposes self.sim / self.session / self.sound_manager / push_modal.
- app_controller.py:48-78 -- push_modal/pop_modal already cascade reset_interaction_state() per CLAUDE.md section 6.4. Section 5.3's claim about ghost-click prevention via existing modal infrastructure is accurate.
- simulation.py:269-303 -- resize_world quoted exactly. reset() does set world_size = DEFAULT_WORLD_SIZE then clear(), so the proposed order (reset() first, then assign new_size) cleanly produces 'fresh sim in resized world.' The double snapshot (one in resize_world, one inside reset()) is benign but worth flagging in execution; recommend the executor either drop the explicit snapshot in resize_world (reset() already snapshots) or accept the harmless duplicate.
- ui/brush_tool.py exists; grep confirms zero 'ui.brush_tool' imports in the active codebase. Orphan classification is correct.
- Existing dialog pattern (SaveAsNewDialog at ui_widgets.py:1478) gives the executor a clean template (rect, done/cancelled flags, handle_event/draw, in-dialog button hit-testing, KEYDOWN absorption) for ConfirmDialog.

Observation for the executor (not blocking):
The new modal will need a modal_type wired into input_handler._attempt_handle_modals (ui/input_handler.py:225-283). The current dispatcher has explicit completion-logic branches per modal_type ('context_menu', 'prop_dialog', 'anim_dialog'); 'save_as_new_dialog' is handled in actions.update(). For 'confirm_resize_dialog', the cleanest fit is to follow the SaveAsNewDialog pattern -- have ConfirmDialog set done/cancelled flags, fire the on_confirm/on_cancel callback inside handle_event, and let close_modal() pop it -- so no new modal_type branch is needed in _attempt_handle_modals. Either path is acceptable; the CR doesn't dictate, which is fine, but the executor should land on one explicit choice and the smoke test in section 8 will catch it.

UI-domain alignment:
- Air Gap: confirmed clean. resize_world is a Simulation method, not a tool; the dialog uses existing push_modal infrastructure; no tool reaches past ToolContext.
- Modal stack: existing infrastructure is reused; reset_interaction_state cascade prevents ghost-clicks (CLAUDE.md section 6.4 satisfied).
- Focus hygiene: no new focus-bearing widgets (ConfirmDialog has buttons only, no input fields), so on_focus_lost is not required.
- Generic by design: section 5.2's 'reusable for any future destructive action' is well-placed -- aligns with the protocol/generalization principle in CLAUDE.md section 9 Pre-Flight.
- The Resize World fix recovers a shipped-but-broken feature; UX adds a single click to a destructive operation. Both are net wins for the user.

Pre-review PASS. Frontmatter title 'Resize World UX fixes and brush_tool orphan deletion' present and matches H1. revision_summary describes Cycle 5 corrections (removed redundant outer snapshot() call in resize_world per tu_sim blocker; added Cancel default-focus to ConfirmDialog spec per bu preference). All template sections 1-12 present and correctly structured; section numbering consistent; no {{}} placeholders remain (square-bracket execution placeholders correctly retained). Scope is narrow and well-defined — three items (InputField visibility fix, confirmation dialog + full reset, brush_tool.py orphan deletion) with bundling rationale documented in sections 1 and 2.1. Implementation Plan phases 9.1-9.8 trace cleanly to the 12 EIs in Section 10. Phase 9.2 correctly marked Not Applicable since no requirements change. Section 7 impact assessment is internally consistent: 7.1 files-affected matches 2.3 and Section 5; 7.2 declares only RTM modified with RS unchanged at v3.0; 7.4 Development Controls and 7.5 Qualified State Continuity present (code CR). Verified all source-of-truth claims against the codebase: flow_state_app.py:109 contains InputField(0,0,0,0) as claimed; ui_manager.py:286 contains the proper-dimensions fallback; simulation.py:269 resize_world structure matches the Before block in 5.4 and reset() at simulation.py:292 does call snapshot() internally (confirms the duplicate-snapshot defect Cycle 5 corrects); app_controller.py:180 action_resize_world matches the Before block in 5.3; brush_tool.py exists with no remaining importers (orphan claim verified); existing dialog classes (MaterialDialog, RotationDialog, SaveAsNewDialog, AnimationDialog) exist in ui_widgets.py supporting the modeled-on-existing-dialogs claim; push_modal infrastructure already in use 5+ times in app_controller.py. SDLC prerequisite states verified: SDLC-FLOW-RS at v3.0 EFFECTIVE and SDLC-FLOW-RTM at v2.0 EFFECTIVE, matching the 7.5 Before CR row. References (Section 12) accurate. Reviewers tu_ui (UI widgets, app controller, app initialization, brush_tool deletion), tu_sim (engine/simulation.py resize_world full-reset semantics), and bu (user-facing UX: confirmation dialog, destructive-confirm focus convention, mental-model claim) assigned to cover affected domains.

User-facing impact is strongly net-positive on all three items. Item 1 (InputField visibility) restores a feature the user cannot currently access through the intended path. Item 2 (confirmation dialog) addresses a destructive-default UX red flag: the current silent wipe is unrecoverable via Ctrl+Z (per Section 3) and a careful user can lose substantial work to a single accidental click. The Section 5.2 spec — generic ConfirmDialog with destructive=True making Cancel the default focus, plus the count>0 skip in Section 5.3 — handles this correctly without adding friction to the no-particles case. Item 3 (orphan deletion) is invisible to users. The Section 8 integration verification covers the visibility, no-particles fast path, cancel path, confirm path, and post-resize state checks I would want exercised.

One non-blocking observation on Section 6 mental-model framing: the 'Resize implies fresh canvas' argument is defensible, and the dialog message in Section 5.3 honestly warns that physics settings will return to defaults, so the user is informed and protected. However, a user who has carefully tuned gravity / damping / target_temp over a long session and just wants more room may be surprised that those settings reset alongside the particle wipe. The dialog warning + Cancel default focus mitigate this, and a future CR could revisit the resize-vs-reset coupling if user feedback warrants — not a blocker for this CR.

Pre-review request-updates from TU-SIM. Specs in §§5.1-5.3 and §5.5 are sound for the simulation domain. The §5.4 simulation.py change has one open issue I want addressed before recommending.

ISSUE 1 (blocking, simulation-domain): Static/tethered atom loss after resize, no compile recovery.

Per CLAUDE.md §2.2 / §5.4, the Compiler bridges CAD geometry to two atom flavors in the simulation: static atoms (is_static=1) for walls and tethered atoms (is_static=3) for dynamic-entity coupling. Both flavors are wiped by the proposed reset() call inside resize_world() (reset -> clear -> count=0).

After the proposed change, when a user resizes the world with CAD entities present:
- Walls disappear from physics (collisions stop working)
- Tethered atoms vanish; the two-way CAD<->Physics coupling silently breaks
- Recovery only happens when something else dirties topology — Scene.update() step 1 (TOPOLOGY CHECK at scene.py:293) only fires rebuild() when _topology_dirty is set, and resize_world does not set it

Note: this is also a latent issue in the current (pre-CR) code — clear(snapshot=False) on the existing path has the same effect. So §5.4 does not introduce the defect, but it does not fix it either, and it touches the same call site.

The established precedent for 'wipe + recompile' is action_clear_particles at app_controller.py:174-178, which calls sim.clear() followed by self.scene.rebuild() to restore static atoms. The new _do_resize_world helper should follow that same pattern. Scene already exposes rebuild() (scene.py:421).

Recommended fix: in §5.3, _do_resize_world should call self.scene.rebuild() after self.sim.resize_world(val) so static and tethered atoms are recompiled into the resized world. This restores domain coherence and matches the established 'destructive-wipe followed by rebuild' convention.

ISSUE 2 (non-blocking, simulation-domain note): The §5.4 cycle 5 correction (removing the outer snapshot) is correct. With the proposed shape — reset() then assign world_size — exactly one snapshot is pushed (from inside reset(), simulation.py:292). Verified against the current snapshot()/clear()/reset() implementations at simulation.py:191-303.

ISSUE 3 (non-blocking, surgical-precision note): A subtler approach would be to just have resize_world() reset only what 'world resize' semantically requires (size + particles) and leave physics tuning intact. The current CR justification (§6 'Mental-model alignment') argues for the full-reset semantics, which is a defensible UX call. I am not blocking on this — the full-reset story is coherent if the rebuild step (issue 1) is added — but flagging that it is a UX choice masquerading as a bug fix, and the more conservative read of 'fix the silent wipe' is to keep clear() semantics and just add the dialog.

Once Issue 1 is addressed (by adding the scene.rebuild() call to _do_resize_world in §5.3), I will recommend.

TU-UI pre-review. Verified: (1) flow_state_app.py:109 InputField(0,0,0,0) defect; (2) simulation.py:269-275 destructive resize_world; (3) reset() snapshots internally (§5.4 Cycle 5 fix is correct); (4) ui/brush_tool.py orphan with zero callers; (5) config.scale/DEFAULT_WORLD_SIZE valid via core.config re-export. Five UI-domain items to address before execution: [1] BLOCKING - dialog dispatch pattern divergence: §5.2 ConfirmDialog uses on_confirm/on_cancel callbacks but every existing dialog in ui_widgets.py (MaterialDialog, RotationDialog, SaveAsNewDialog, AnimationDialog) uses the polled-flag pattern dispatched centrally in input_handler._attempt_handle_modals() (lines 258-281, keyed on modal_type) and actions.update() (lines 585-596). CR must specify integration approach. Strong preference: conform to existing pattern - expose done/confirmed flags on ConfirmDialog and add a 'confirm_resize_dialog' branch in input_handler._attempt_handle_modals() that calls _do_resize_world on confirm and close_modal in both cases. CR-114 is a bug-fix CR, not the right vehicle for introducing a callback-based dialog idiom; if we want callback dialogs, that warrants its own CR sweeping the existing four. [2] BLOCKING - click-outside-to-dismiss: input_handler.py lines 248-255 centralizes click-outside dismissal, but unrecognized modal_types fall through to actions.close_modal() which would not fire on_cancel. For a destructive-confirm dialog, click-outside should route through Cancel (status: 'Resize cancelled'). Add a 'confirm_resize_dialog' branch handling this explicitly. [3] §5.2 wording: signature is draw(screen, font) per actions.draw_overlays line 602, not draw(screen). Minor fix. [4] §5.2 destructive=True keyboard wiring: please make Enter->Cancel and Escape->Cancel explicit (currently 'Enter key, where applicable' is parenthetical). The keyboard semantics are load-bearing for the destructive-default UX claim. [5] §8 step 3 confirmation-flow test should add keyboard substeps: 'Press Enter -> verify Cancel fires (not Confirm) when destructive=True' and 'Press Escape -> verify Cancel fires'. This validates the §5.2 destructive-default claim. Items 1 and 4 are blocking; 2, 3, 5 are spec-tightening that should be resolved in the same revision cycle.

Pre-review COMPLIANT.

Frontmatter verified: title='Resize World UX fixes and brush_tool orphan deletion'; revision_summary itemizes 7 cycle-6 corrections, all of which I traced into the document body (polled-flag ConfirmDialog at lines 107-110; scene.rebuild() in _do_resize_world at line 171 with rationale at line 184; input_handler.py added to 2.3 and 7.1; Enter/Escape keyboard wiring at lines 119-121; section 8 step 3 keyboard substeps at lines 335-339; EI-5 extended to cover dispatcher branch).

Required sections: all 12 present. Substantive content has no TBD/TODO/FIXME/XXX. Execution-table placeholders ([SUMMARY], [Pass/Fail], [PERFORMER]-[DATE], [EXECUTION_SUMMARY]) are the standard SOP-004 templates filled during execution and are expected at pre-review.

Internal consistency: 2.3 file list matches 5.1-5.5 change descriptions and 7.1 impact table. EI-3 through EI-7 map 1:1 to sections 5.1-5.5; EI-1/2/8/9/10/11/12 cover baseline, env, qualification, integration verification, RTM, merge, post-baseline. Full coverage.

Cross-references independently verified against the codebase: flow_state_app.py:109 is indeed InputField(0,0,0,0); app_controller.py:174-178 is the scene.rebuild() precedent in action_clear_particles; simulation.py:292 confirms reset() calls snapshot() internally (so the order swap in 5.4 is correct and the outer snapshot must be removed); SaveAsNewDialog at ui_widgets.py:1478 confirms the done/cancelled polled-flag precedent; grep for 'from ui.brush_tool' / 'import ui.brush_tool' / 'from .brush_tool' returned zero matches across flow-state, confirming the orphan claim.

VR column empty across all EIs - consistent with the CR-111/112/113 inspection-based pattern (Lead-witnessed interactive smoke in EI-9 substitutes for formal VRs since flow-state has no automated test suite). Section 8 explicitly documents this approach.

RS non-revision justified: REQ-FS-UI-001 (Hierarchical UI Tree) and REQ-FS-UI-002 (Input Chain - Modal precedes Global) are high-level architectural; the InputField visibility fix, ConfirmDialog, and physics reset all live within the existing requirement surface without changing requirement shape. Section 7.5 Qualified State Continuity table internally consistent (RS v3.0 unchanged, RTM v2.0 to v3.0, FLOW-STATE-1.1 to 1.2).

Recommend proceeding to pre-approval. Reviewer assignment for the post-execution cycle should include tu_ui (input_handler dispatcher, ConfirmDialog widget, InputField fix, app_controller modal flow), tu_sim (simulation.resize_world reset behavior), tu_scene (scene.rebuild() coupling), and bu (user-visible behavior change: confirmation dialog, destructive-Enter UX).

Pre-review cycle 7 (procedural reset). COMPLIANT.

CONTEXT: This cycle exists to re-engage tu_ui, tu_sim, tu_scene, and bu on the cycle 6 substantive corrections. Cycle 6 closed with only QA RECOMMEND because no TUs were assigned in time and the sole-reviewer auto-close fired. QA pre-review: RECOMMEND. Awaiting tu_ui, tu_sim, tu_scene, and bu reviews.

BU pre-review: RECOMMEND.

User Impact (positive):
1. InputField visibility fix restores a feature the user currently cannot use at all (input field rendered at 0x0). Pure win.
2. Confirmation dialog for destructive resize eliminates a Destructive Default UX red flag — a user who has built up a particle simulation will no longer lose work to an accidental click.
3. Skipping the dialog when sim.count == 0 is the correct call: no friction added to the benign case. Avoids Click Fatigue.
4. destructive=True making Enter route to Cancel directly defeats the 'hasty Enter destroys state' failure mode. This is thoughtful UX.
5. Click-outside-dismiss, Escape, and explicit Cancel button all routing to Cancel match user expectations for destructive confirmations.
6. Status bar feedback ('Resize cancelled' / 'World Resized: N') maintains the existing feedback convention — no Missing Feedback issue.

Justification Alignment:
- Section 6 cleanly maps each fix to a real user-facing problem (invisible input, silent data loss, mental-model mismatch).
- The §5.4 'full physics reset' is a behavioral change, not just a bug fix, but the dialog message explicitly tells the user 'All N particles will be cleared and physics settings will return to defaults' — consent is informed and explicit. Acceptable.

Consistency:
- ConfirmDialog modeled on existing dialog classes (SaveAsNewDialog, MaterialDialog, RotationDialog, AnimationDialog) preserves UI design language.
- Generic-by-design with the destructive flag sets up a reusable pattern that future destructive actions can adopt — improves long-term UX consistency across the app.

Integration verification (§8) is well-constructed; the explicit Enter-as-Cancel test proves the destructive-default UX actually works rather than just claiming it.

No blocking UX concerns. No request-updates.

Pre-review recommend. Verified the UI-domain claims against the live code: (1) InputField(0,0,0,0) bug at flow_state_app.py:109 is real and the §5.1 fix mirrors the existing ui_manager.py:286 fallback exactly, preserving the Session-attached field pattern across all rebuild paths (lines 127/232/334). (2) ConfirmDialog spec (§5.2) follows the established polled-flag idiom (done/cancelled, handle_event/update/draw/rect/visible) consistent with SaveAsNewDialog at ui_widgets.py:1478; the destructive=True Enter->Cancel rule is sound and load-bearing per the explicit justification. (3) Dispatcher branches in §5.3 slot cleanly into _attempt_handle_modals at input_handler.py:225-283 alongside the existing save_as_new_dialog (click-outside) and anim_dialog (completion polling) precedents. (4) push_modal at app_controller.py:48-61 already cascades root.reset_interaction_state(), confirming the ghost-click protection cited. (5) scene.rebuild() after sim.reset() correctly mirrors action_clear_particles at app_controller.py:174-178 — required because Simulation.reset() invokes clear() internally and wipes static/tethered atoms. (6) Grepped flow-state for brush_tool references: zero matches outside the file itself; BrushTool is at ui/tools.py:124; orphan deletion safe. No Air Gap or ToolContext concerns — Resize World originates from a Button routed through AppController, the conformant path. One non-blocking implementation note: §5.3 doesn't specify the dialog's center-of-screen positioning arithmetic; the implementer should mirror how existing dialog callers compute (x, y), which is within EI scope.

Orchestration-domain pre-review: RECOMMEND with one minor implementation-detail clarification suggested.

VERIFIED CLAIMS (all accurate against current code):
- flow_state_app.py:109 constructs InputField(0,0,0,0); ui_manager.py:286 fallback exists with the proposed dimensions.
- simulation.py:269-275 resize_world() current shape matches; reset() at 290-303 already calls snapshot() internally and clears all particles including static (is_static=1) and tethered (is_static=3) atoms.
- Scene.rebuild() at scene.py:421-429 invokes Compiler.rebuild() and snap_tethered_atoms_to_anchors() — the correct path to restore Compiler-emitted atoms post-reset.
- action_clear_particles (app_controller.py:174-178) is the cited precedent for the sim.clear() + scene.rebuild() pattern; the CR mirrors it faithfully.
- No 'from ui.brush_tool' or 'import ui.brush_tool' anywhere in flow-state — orphan condition confirmed.
- Modal infrastructure (push_modal/close_modal, polled-flag completion in input_handler._attempt_handle_modals and app_controller.update) is all in place and well-described.

ORCHESTRATION JUDGMENT:
1. Command integrity: Correctly stays on the physics-snapshot undo path (consistent with §3.3 dichotomy and action_clear_particles precedent). The removal of the outer snapshot() call in §5.4 is essential — reset() already snapshots, so retaining the outer call would require double Ctrl+Z. Clean fix.
2. Two-way CAD↔Physics coupling: The most consequential orchestration point. reset() wipes both is_static=1 and is_static=3 atoms; without scene.rebuild() the coupling silently breaks until the next topology dirtying. §5.3 names both atom flavors and inserts scene.rebuild() in _do_resize_world — correct.
3. State ownership: InputField fix preserves Session-attached pattern (transient UI state, correct per §4.2). pending_value stash on the dialog is appropriate transient state on a transient object. No model mutation introduced.
4. Air Gap: No regression. Tools are not involved; this is a controller-level action invoking sim/scene methods through the existing channels.

MINOR CLARIFICATION SUGGESTED (not blocking):
§5.3's modal-completion polling 'around line 273, after anim_dialog' fires on event-driven completions (Enter/Escape/button clicks) but the click-outside-to-dismiss arm sets done=True then returns True from line 255 within the same tick. As written it implicitly relies on a subsequent event re-routing to the (still-stacked) modal to trigger apply_resize_confirm. The save_as_new_dialog precedent instead polls done from actions.update() (app_controller.py:593) on the next frame. Consider either (a) calling actions.apply_resize_confirm(modal) directly from the click-outside branch in input_handler, or (b) moving the completion polling into actions.update() to match the save_as_new_dialog precedent exactly. Either resolution is fine — the CR should pick one and state it explicitly so the implementer doesn't land click-outside completion on the wrong tick.

No orchestration concerns block pre-approval.

Pre-review RECOMMEND from tu_sim. Simulation-domain analysis:

(1) resize_world() full-reset semantics (Sec 5.4): correct. reset() snapshots state internally before mutating defaults, then clear(snapshot=False) zeroes particle arrays - the proposed ordering (reset() then world_size = new_size) yields a single undo entry per resize and cleanly eliminates the prior bare self.snapshot() call. Cleaner than the current shape and behaviorally equivalent on the snapshot count.

(2) scene.rebuild() after sim.resize_world() (Sec 5.3): necessary and correctly justified. clear() zeros is_static/pos_x/pos_y/vel_x/vel_y and sets count=0, destroying all static (is_static=1) and tethered (is_static=3) atoms emitted by the Compiler. Without rebuild(), post-resize the physics has no representation of CAD walls and the two-way CAD<->Physics coupling silently breaks until topology is dirtied by some other path. The pattern mirrors the existing precedent at app_controller.py:174-178 (action_clear_particles).

(3) Tether array hygiene: not a concern. tether_entity_idx, tether_local_pos, tether_stiffness, joint_ids, atom_sigma, atom_eps_sqrt, atom_color are not zeroed by clear(), but every physics kernel call slices these by [:self.count] (e.g., simulation.py:447-449, 730). After Compiler.rebuild() re-emits tethered atoms starting from index 0, the live region of the arrays is always fresh; stale entries beyond count are unreachable.

(4) brush_tool.py orphan deletion (Sec 5.5): verified no 'from ui.brush_tool' or 'import ui.brush_tool' anywhere in flow-state. The active BrushTool in ui/tools.py routes through ctx.paint_particles/ctx.erase_particles which delegate to engine/particle_brush.py - deletion does not break the simulation-side brush surface.

(5) Two-way coupling integrity: explicitly preserved. The CR recognizes both is_static=1 and is_static=3 atom flavors and invokes scene.rebuild() (which calls Compiler.rebuild()) to restore both. Tether bookkeeping (tether_entity_idx/tether_local_pos/tether_stiffness) is fully reconstructed.

(6) Scope completeness from sim side: only one caller of resize_world() (app_controller.action_resize_world via input_handler.ui_action_map[buttons['resize']]) - scope is fully bounded and the CR covers it.

Non-blocking observation (informational, not a request-update): Simulation.snapshot() at simulation.py:191-208 captures count, pos_x/y, vel_x/y, is_static, atom_sigma, atom_eps_sqrt, atom_color, world_size - but reset() also mutates dt, gravity, target_temp, damping, use_boundaries, sigma, epsilon, skin_distance. So a Ctrl+Z after a resize-with-particles will restore particles and world_size but leave physics tuning at defaults. This is a pre-existing asymmetry in reset()/snapshot() - not introduced by this CR. If symmetric undo is wanted, that is a separate cleanup CR against snapshot()/restore_physics_state(). Not a blocker for CR-114.

No simulation-domain defects, no architectural concerns, no scope gaps. Recommend pre-approval.
